terms.definition: Sudden neurological deficit caused by interruption of cerebral blood supply, either ischaemic or haemorrhagic.
terms.exampleSentence: The patient presented with sudden left-sided weakness suggestive of an acute ischaemic stroke.
terms.contextNotes: Time-critical; thrombolysis window is typically 4.5 hours.

terms.definition: Brief episode of neurological dysfunction caused by focal cerebral ischaemia without infarction.
terms.exampleSentence: She had a transient ischaemic attack lasting twenty minutes with full recovery.
terms.contextNotes: Resolves within 24 hours by definition; high stroke risk.

terms.definition: Chronic neurological disorder characterised by recurrent unprovoked seizures.
terms.exampleSentence: He was diagnosed with epilepsy after two unprovoked tonic-clonic seizures.
terms.contextNotes: First-line therapies include sodium valproate and lamotrigine.

terms.definition: Progressive neurodegenerative disorder featuring tremor, rigidity, bradykinesia and postural instability.
terms.exampleSentence: The elderly gentleman with Parkinson's disease has worsening bradykinesia and shuffling gait.
terms.contextNotes: Caused by dopaminergic neuron loss in the substantia nigra.

terms.definition: Autoimmune demyelinating disease of the central nervous system causing relapsing neurological symptoms.
terms.exampleSentence: Her optic neuritis was the first presentation of multiple sclerosis on MRI.
terms.contextNotes: MRI shows periventricular white-matter plaques.

terms.definition: Recurrent moderate-to-severe headache, often unilateral and pulsatile, with photophobia and nausea.
terms.exampleSentence: She takes sumatriptan for acute migraine attacks with aura.
terms.contextNotes: Triptans are first-line acute therapy in moderate-severe attacks.

terms.definition: Inflammation of the meninges, most commonly due to bacterial or viral infection.
terms.exampleSentence: The child with neck stiffness and a non-blanching rash was treated for meningococcal meningitis.
terms.contextNotes: Empirical IV ceftriaxone given immediately if suspected.

terms.definition: Inflammation of the brain parenchyma, most often viral, presenting with altered mental state and fever.
terms.exampleSentence: Herpes simplex encephalitis was treated with intravenous aciclovir.
terms.contextNotes: MRI typically shows temporal lobe involvement in HSV.

terms.definition: Bleeding into the subarachnoid space, classically from a ruptured berry aneurysm.
terms.exampleSentence: A sudden thunderclap headache prompted CT imaging for subarachnoid haemorrhage.
terms.contextNotes: Nimodipine reduces cerebral vasospasm risk.

terms.definition: Acute idiopathic unilateral lower motor neuron facial nerve paralysis.
terms.exampleSentence: The patient woke with facial drooping consistent with Bell's palsy and was prescribed prednisolone.
terms.contextNotes: Oral prednisolone within 72 hours improves recovery.

terms.definition: Acquired progressive decline in cognition affecting daily functioning, often due to Alzheimer's disease.
terms.exampleSentence: His worsening short-term memory loss led to a diagnosis of Alzheimer's dementia.
terms.contextNotes: Donepezil is first-line for mild-moderate Alzheimer's.

terms.definition: Procedure to obtain cerebrospinal fluid by inserting a needle into the lumbar subarachnoid space.
terms.exampleSentence: A lumbar puncture confirmed elevated white cells consistent with bacterial meningitis.
terms.contextNotes: CT brain first if signs of raised intracranial pressure.

terms.definition: Involuntary rhythmic oscillatory movement of a body part.
terms.exampleSentence: His resting tremor of the right hand was suggestive of Parkinson's disease.
terms.contextNotes: Resting tremor suggests Parkinson's; intention tremor suggests cerebellar disease.

terms.definition: Acquired disorder of language production or comprehension due to brain injury, commonly stroke.
terms.exampleSentence: The post-stroke patient developed expressive aphasia and was referred to speech therapy.
terms.contextNotes: Broca's lesions cause expressive; Wernicke's cause receptive aphasia.

terms.definition: Broad-spectrum antiepileptic drug effective for generalised and focal seizures.
terms.exampleSentence: Sodium valproate was avoided in this woman of childbearing age due to teratogenicity.
terms.contextNotes: Highly teratogenic; contraindicated in pregnancy without specialist plan.

terms.definition: Chronic retrograde flow of gastric contents into the oesophagus causing symptoms or mucosal injury.
terms.exampleSentence: His chronic heartburn responded well to omeprazole for gastro-oesophageal reflux disease.
terms.contextNotes: Long-standing GORD predisposes to Barrett's oesophagus.

terms.definition: Mucosal break in the stomach or duodenum, commonly due to H. pylori or NSAIDs.
terms.exampleSentence: Endoscopy confirmed peptic ulcer disease and triple therapy was commenced for H. pylori.
terms.contextNotes: H. pylori eradication uses PPI plus two antibiotics for 7 days.

terms.definition: Chronic immune-mediated inflammation of the gut, comprising Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis.
terms.exampleSentence: Her bloody diarrhoea and weight loss raised suspicion of inflammatory bowel disease.
terms.contextNotes: Faecal calprotectin distinguishes IBD from IBS.

terms.definition: Chronic relapsing inflammatory bowel disease affecting the colonic mucosa, typically continuous from the rectum.
terms.exampleSentence: His ulcerative colitis flare was managed with intravenous hydrocortisone.
terms.contextNotes: Increases long-term colorectal cancer risk; surveillance colonoscopy needed.

terms.definition: Transmural granulomatous inflammation that may affect any segment of the gastrointestinal tract.
terms.exampleSentence: Crohn's disease of the terminal ileum caused recurrent right iliac fossa pain.
terms.contextNotes: Skip lesions and granulomas distinguish it from UC.

terms.definition: Diffuse hepatic fibrosis with regenerative nodules, the end stage of chronic liver disease.
terms.exampleSentence: His alcoholic cirrhosis was complicated by ascites and oesophageal varices.
terms.contextNotes: Child-Pugh and MELD scores assess severity.

terms.definition: Inflammation of the pancreas, most commonly due to gallstones or alcohol.
terms.exampleSentence: Acute pancreatitis was diagnosed by epigastric pain and a markedly raised serum lipase.
terms.contextNotes: Serum lipase is more specific than amylase for diagnosis.

terms.definition: Acute inflammation of the gallbladder, usually secondary to cystic duct obstruction by gallstones.
terms.exampleSentence: Right upper quadrant tenderness with a positive Murphy's sign suggested acute cholecystitis.
terms.contextNotes: Early laparoscopic cholecystectomy is preferred treatment.

terms.definition: Inflammation or infection of colonic diverticula, presenting with left iliac fossa pain and fever.
terms.exampleSentence: She was admitted with left lower abdominal pain and a CT diagnosis of acute diverticulitis.
terms.contextNotes: Sigmoid colon is the most commonly affected site.

terms.definition: Functional bowel disorder with abdominal pain and altered bowel habit without structural cause.
terms.exampleSentence: After a normal colonoscopy she was diagnosed with irritable bowel syndrome.
terms.contextNotes: Diagnosis of exclusion using Rome IV criteria.

terms.definition: Vomiting of blood, indicating upper gastrointestinal haemorrhage.
terms.exampleSentence: Massive haematemesis from oesophageal varices required urgent endoscopy and band ligation.
terms.contextNotes: Glasgow-Blatchford score guides need for urgent endoscopy.

terms.definition: Black tarry stool produced by digested blood from upper gastrointestinal bleeding.
terms.exampleSentence: Melaena and hypotension prompted urgent endoscopy for a bleeding duodenal ulcer.
terms.contextNotes: Indicates blood loss above the ileocaecal valve.

terms.definition: Endoscopic examination of the colon and terminal ileum using a flexible fibreoptic scope.
terms.exampleSentence: Colonoscopy revealed a sigmoid polyp which was removed for histology.
terms.contextNotes: Bowel preparation and consent for polypectomy required beforehand.

terms.definition: Proton pump inhibitor that reduces gastric acid secretion.
terms.exampleSentence: Omeprazole 40 mg once daily was prescribed for her reflux symptoms.
terms.contextNotes: Long-term use may impair magnesium and B12 absorption.

terms.definition: Pathological accumulation of fluid within the peritoneal cavity, commonly from cirrhosis.
terms.exampleSentence: Tense ascites was drained therapeutically by paracentesis.
terms.contextNotes: Spontaneous bacterial peritonitis is a key complication.
